subject: ela
title: Context Clues
content:
Context Clues

What Are Context Clues?

Context clues are words around an unfamiliar word that help you figure out its meaning. You don't always need a dictionary!

Types of Context Clues

1. Definition/Explanation
The word is directly defined in the sentence.

"The benevolent king, or one who is kind and generous..."

2. Example
Examples are given to show meaning.
"Fruits, such as apples, oranges, and bananas..."

3. Synonym
A similar word gives a clue.

"The child seemed terrified (very frightened) and hid."

4. Contrast
An opposite word or idea gives a clue.

"Unlike her shy sister, Emma was very outgoing."

How to Use Context Clues

Step 1: Look at the sentence with the unfamiliar word
Step 2: Find clues nearby (definitions, examples, synonyms, contrasts)
Step 3: Use those clues to guess the meaning
Step 4: Check if your guess makes sense

Why Context Clues Matter

• Helps you read faster
• Builds vocabulary naturally
• Works when you don't have a dictionary
• Makes you a better reader!

Practice

"The weather was temperate today — not too hot and not too cold."

Temperate most likely means:
• Moderate (in the middle)
• Based on the contrast clue in "not too hot and not too cold"

"Her gregarious personality made her the center of every party."

Gregarious most likely means:
• Outgoing, social
• Based on example - "center of every party"

questions.prompt: "She was articulate, able to speak clearly and eloquently..." What type of context clue is this?
"Unlike his brother, Marcus was very shy." What does 'shy' mean here, based on the contrast?

choices: Words around an unfamiliar word that help meaning
The first word in a sentence

questions.prompt: "The weather was inclement — rain and strong winds all day." What does 'inclement' mean?
Using context clues is helpful because...
choices: You can figure out words without a dictionary
questions.hint: Context clues are about understanding without looking up
